version_status: in progress; last released 1.10.4 (see CHANGELOG.md). The brief's '1.1' and component-status.html's 'v1.8' header are stale internal labels — the changelog is authoritative.
objective: A grayscale, tokens-first wireframe and mid-fidelity system for client work in healthcare and higher-ed. Used as the constant starting point: paired with a per-project brief, an agent composes project-specific functional prototypes from these parts only. NOTE: today this repo is a component library + demos; the 'generation system' described in PREVIEW-SYSTEM-BRIEF.md (machine-driven composition, an Experiences tier, project scaffolding) is the destination, not the current state.

_meta.purpose: Machine-readable inventory. This file is an ACCURATE MIRROR OF WHAT EXISTS IN SOURCE TODAY, not the vision in PREVIEW-SYSTEM-BRIEF.md.
regenerated_from: css/wire.css (import order = the canonical component list)
css/tokens.css (the only file with raw values)
css/components/*.css (verified class names, variants, modifiers, states)
docs/*.html (documentation home per component; page-shells; component-status lifecycle table)
pages/*.html (composed_of derived from real class usage, not guessed)
_meta.every_class_verified_against: css/components/*.css and css/{base,layout,utilities}.css. No inferred class names remain.
_meta.component_count_reconciliation: README and component-status.html advertise '30 components' — a curated logical count. This manifest lists 43 authoring entries because it splits multi-component files into their real parts (nav.css -> topnav/megamenu/sidenav/inpagenav/drawer; form.css -> field/search/choice/form-layout; list.css -> linked-list/deflist/article-list; media.css -> media/gallery; help-bar.css -> help-bar/phone-link). Plus 6 doc_chrome entries (status-pill + doc-header/nav/shell/pager + app-shell) that are the docs-site's own furniture, not client-composition parts. There are 38 partial files in css/components/ (31 wireframe + 7 doc-chrome: the 6 entried sheets plus doc-helpers).
_meta.corrections_vs_prior_manifest: Headline fixes: wire-btn->wire-button, wire-two-col->wire-twocol, wire-pullquote->wire-quote, wire-helpbar->wire-help-bar, wire-phone->wire-phone-link, wire-textsize->wire-text-size; tokens.source docs/tokens.css->css/tokens.css; added gray-100 and the borders/shadows/motion token groups; added the entire nav family + modal/toast/banner/empty/skeleton/stepper/tooltip/citation-list/status-pill; removed the two fabricated 'experiences' (the Experiences tier does not exist yet); fixed callout, badge, tag, list, form, card, hero, table variant/class sets; corrected text-size options.

constraints: Grayscale only. Hierarchy comes from weight, scale, and space — never hue.
Tokens only. Every value references css/tokens.css. Nothing is hardcoded.
No new tokens, no new components, no inline styles. Compose from what exists.
Content variations, not style overrides. Swap copy and structure; never restyle.
Plain HTML + CSS, no build step. A file must work when opened directly in a browser. Add js/wire.js (defer) only if the page uses an interactive behavior.
Every component class starts with `wire-`; every utility with `u-`. States use `.is-*`; JS hooks use `data-wire-*`.
WCAG 2.2 AA: contrast tested, visible focus on everything, targets >=44px, reduced-motion respected, labels on all inputs.
Healthcare audiences skew older — prefer larger text defaults and keep the help-bar / text-size / phone affordances available where appropriate.

category_taxonomy.note: The nine component categories shown in the rail and components/index.html (Actions, Forms & inputs, Selection & status, Containment & overlays, Navigation, Sections, Content & display, Messaging & feedback, Utilities), plus foundation for type and doc-chrome for docs-site furniture. Used for the `category` field on each component. The ordered, labelled rail tree lives in `nav`.

evidence.render: docs/research.html (the human-readable surface of the same evidence)
evidence.rule: Evidence is the sixth, cross-cutting dimension every node carries. A node's `rationale` cites citations.json by id. Never invent a citation; never present an unverified one as fact; never promote a claim above the tier its evidence supports. Curated per EVIDENCE-MODEL.md §6: `rationale` covers decisions that are client-questioned, non-obvious, or carry accessibility/legal weight — not every node. Nodes without a `rationale` block are still linked to evidence via each citation's `supports` array (the reverse index).

system_rationale.decision: Grayscale forces hierarchy from weight/scale/proximity/contrast — the perceptual mechanisms the cited research describes; the system demonstrates what it cites
Grayscale ramp meets WCAG AA contrast at named steps

tokens.note: Raw values live only here. tokens.css aliases the gray ramp into semantic color tokens; client overlays re-alias these (see extension_lanes).

foundations.notes: Typographic primitives + the focus-ring/skip-link contract. Body capped ~70ch via .wire-prose. wire-quote here is the base blockquote; the pull-quote component is css/components/quote.css.
Print stylesheet; expands <details> for print. No authoring classes.

rationale.decision: Body 16px default, never below 14px in flow
Max line length 50-75ch (70ch cap)
text-wrap: balance on headings
One descriptive h1 per page; ordered headings

components.class: wire-h1 .. wire-h6, wire-display, wire-lead, wire-eyebrow, wire-prose, wire-caption
components.notes: Lives in base.css, not a components/ partial. Lead is muted so it doesn't compete with the heading.

rationale.decision: >=44px primary target size
Three-tier action hierarchy (primary/secondary/ghost)
Hover affordance via background fill, not underline-only
Visible focus ring with 2px offset
Loading state announces busy

components.a11y: Min 44px target, visible focus ring, loading state announces busy. Primary is the unmodified base class. Icon-leading/trailing is DOM order of wire-button__icon, not a class.
components.notes: Canonical M3 doc template is components/buttons/index.html (tabbed Overview/Specs/Guidelines/Accessibility with hash deep-linking).
components.label: Button

rationale.decision: Grayscale fill-density variants with a load-bearing text label; status is never carried by color alone
A badge draws attention through contrast of form and proximity to its host, not color

components.composable: Leaf; composes into cards, tables, and list items, and (as --count via wire-badge-anchor) onto a focusable host such as an icon button.
components.notes: Non-interactive. Count overlay (wire-badge--count + wire-badge-anchor): the host carries the accessible name and the visual count is aria-hidden. Prior manifest's 'live' variant does not exist.
components.label: Badge

rationale.decision: Chip / removable-tag pattern
tag--sm holds the 24px AA target floor
Interactive tag is a real button/a; selection exposed via aria-pressed (not fill alone); remove target meets 24px
Filter chips let users narrow and undo a query; controls sized per Fitts's law

components.notes: Two modes: interactive (button/a) and read-only metadata (span). No JS ships; the app wires selection (aria-pressed + .is-selected) and removal. Selected state is a dark accent fill (structural via fill density, not hue). Group with wire-tag-list.
components.label: Tag

rationale.decision: Rules and vertical rhythm separate content; use sparingly and let spacing carry most grouping

components.notes: Horizontal (an <hr>, semantic separator) and vertical (decorative inline rule). Labeled divider is a separate class wire-divider-labeled, not a --labeled modifier.
components.label: Divider

rationale.decision: Labels above inputs, never placeholder-as-label
Inline error adjacent to field via aria-describedby
Required-field asterisk + visible label note
Input border clears 3:1 non-text contrast

components.a11y: Label always present; error text tied via aria-describedby; is-invalid + aria-invalid on the control.
components.notes: wire-field is the labeled wrapper around wire-input/wire-textarea/wire-select. Prior manifest treated input/select/textarea as standalone and invented helper/field slots.
components.label: Field

rationale.decision: Leading magnifier icon for search

components.notes: Three distinct classes, not one wire-choice with modifiers. Label-wrapped pattern.
components.label: Choice

rationale.decision: Single-column form layout by default

components.notes: Form scaffolding: wire-form, wire-form__row (multi-column rows), wire-fieldset + wire-fieldset__legend for grouped choices.
components.label: Form layout

behaviors: Pairs with the mobile drawer: wire-topnav__burger carries data-wire-drawer-open
components.notes: The page-shell common scaffold opens with wire-topnav on every page.
components.label: Top nav

rationale.decision: Mega menu for multi-audience IA fan-out
Trigger stays <a href> for no-JS fallback; JS adds aria-haspopup/expanded

behaviors: data-wire-megamenu (init)
data-wire-megamenu-trigger (the toggle; sets aria-haspopup/aria-expanded)
click-toggle, outside-click close, Escape closes and refocuses trigger (initMegamenu)
components.notes: Trigger remains <a href> for no-JS fallback.
components.label: Mega menu

rationale.decision: Left-rail vertical nav for deep IAs
aria-current=page on the active item

components.notes: Three documented compositions (local / secondary / section tree). wire-sidenav__sublist + __link--parent are beta until used beyond the hospital vertical.
components.label: Side nav

rationale.decision: In-page TOC with scroll-spy on long-form pages
Non-sticky below 48em to preserve reflow

behaviors: data-wire-inpagenav (init)
IntersectionObserver highlights the link for the section in view via .is-current (initInPageNav)
components.notes: Non-sticky below 48em.
components.label: In-page nav

rationale.decision: Modal-dialog pattern with focus trap, Esc, focus restore

behaviors: data-wire-drawer-open="<drawerId>" on the toggle
data-wire-drawer-close on close buttons
focus trap (WCAG 2.1.2), backdrop click closes, Escape closes, body scroll locked, role=dialog + aria-modal set (initDrawer)
components.notes: Mobile navigation drawer. Pairs with wire-topnav__burger.
components.label: Drawer

rationale.decision: Grouping by proximity + shared region
Card pattern for related content
Bordered variant clears 3:1 non-text contrast

components.notes: Content is the unmodified base (no wire-card--content). Modifiers compose: e.g. wire-card wire-card--person wire-card--bordered.
components.label: Card

rationale.decision: Mobile-first hero scaling (step up at md/lg)
The single page h1 lives in the hero

components.notes: Pick by intent: editorial = story/emotion; split = service/CTA pair; centered = single focus; stat = credibility/proof. inverse is a separate modifier (prior manifest's 'stat-led-inverse' = wire-hero--stat wire-hero--inverse). The page h1 lives once, inside the hero.
components.label: Hero

rationale.decision: Icon + title + description grid for landing pages

components.notes: Class is wire-twocol (prior manifest's wire-two-col was wrong). top/bottom control vertical alignment; reverse swaps column order.
components.label: Two-column

rationale.decision: Native <details> disclosure; single-open option

behaviors: data-wire-accordion="single" enables single-open mode (closes siblings on open). Built on native <details>/<summary> (initAccordion)
components.a11y: Native disclosure pattern; keyboard operable for free.
components.label: Accordion

rationale.decision: APG tabs keyboard model (arrows / Home / End)
Parallel peer content shown one panel at a time; follow the conventional tab interaction so it is understood without learning

behaviors: data-wire-tabs (init)
data-wire-tabs-hash (sync active tab with URL hash via History API; deep-linkable, survives back/forward)
ARIA APG tabpanel pattern: roving tabindex, arrow/Home/End keys, aria-selected, aria-controls (initTabs)
components.notes: wire-tabs--sticky is defined in doc-shell.css. The components/buttons M3 template uses data-wire-tabs-hash for #overview/#specs/#guidelines/#accessibility.
components.label: Tabs

rationale.decision: Aside emphasis as a distinct, programmatically-identifiable region

components.notes: Prior manifest's enrolling/navigator/alert-inline were content uses, not real variants. Real variants are subtle/inverse/bordered/inline.
components.label: Callout

rationale.decision: Pull quote for scannability and rhythm

components.notes: Pull-quote. Class is wire-quote (prior manifest's wire-pullquote was wrong).
components.label: Quote

rationale.decision: Vertical chronology with anchored date markers
Decorative placeholders marked aria-hidden

components.notes: Aspect-ratio placeholders. Variants are explicit ratios, not the prior 'ratio-placeholder' guess.
components.label: Media

components.notes: Mosaic variant collapses to a single column on mobile.
components.label: Gallery

rationale.decision: Large clickable rows + descriptive meta for category landings

components.notes: Standalone class (prior manifest's wire-list--linked modifier pattern was wrong).
components.label: Linked list

rationale.decision: APA / Chicago reference-list visual conventions

components.notes: Used on hospital-measure.html and directory.html. Beta until a third page uses it.
components.label: Citation list

rationale.decision: Header scope + programmatic association; tabular figures

components.notes: Comparison modifier is wire-table--comparison (prior manifest's --compare was wrong). wire-table__num applies tabular numerals.
components.label: Table

rationale.decision: Step indicator for multi-step flows (ol + aria-current=step)

components.notes: Multi-step progress indicator. Used in booking/appointment/trial/program flows. Beta until WCAG screen-reader pass.
components.label: Stepper

rationale.decision: Modal-dialog pattern; blocking reserved for justified cases

behaviors: data-wire-modal-open="<modalId>" on the trigger
data-wire-modal-close on close buttons
focus trap, backdrop click closes, Escape closes, restores focus to opener, body scroll locked, role=dialog + aria-modal (initModal)
data-wire-modal-static suppresses backdrop-click dismissal (Esc + close controls still work)
autofocus sets initial focus; aria-describedby auto-wired to the body (initModal)
components.notes: Centered dialog. Beta until SR pass + a third use.
components.label: Modal

rationale.decision: Transient status via a polite live region

behaviors: window.wireToast({title, body, duration}) JS API
declarative: data-wire-toast-trigger + data-toast-title / data-toast-body / data-toast-duration
WCAG 4.1.3 status region, aria-live=polite (initToast)
components.notes: API surface may refine.
components.label: Toast

rationale.decision: Site-level message, dismissable, programmatically distinguishable

behaviors: wire-banner__close removes the closest .wire-banner (initBanner) — no data attribute required
components.label: Banner

rationale.decision: Empty state: explain, reduce confusion, offer recovery

components.notes: Used in 5 demos (404, search, portal, appointment, etc.).
components.label: Empty state

rationale.decision: Loading placeholder reduces perceived wait; shimmer respects reduced-motion

components.a11y: Pairs with aria-busy on the loading container.
components.notes: Loading placeholders.
components.label: Skeleton

rationale.decision: Dismissable / hoverable / persistent hover content; aria-label is the name

behaviors: data-wire-tooltip="<text>" (content)
data-wire-tooltip-placement="top|bottom|left|right"
PURE CSS — no JS init; pseudo-element renders the bubble
components.notes: Attribute-driven, not a class. Long content needs a popover (post-v1.4).
components.label: Tooltip

components.notes: Four variants: standard (default), compact, expanded (closing-CTA), inverse. Always inside <footer>, outside <main>.
components.label: Footer

rationale.decision: Persistent high-contrast help/contact affordance for older adults

components.notes: Persistent 'talk to someone' affordance (NIA Senior-Friendly). Class is wire-help-bar.
components.label: Help bar

rationale.decision: Prominent phone channel for care contact

components.notes: Topnav-ready, tabular numerals, tel: href. Class is wire-phone-link; lives in help-bar.css.
components.label: Phone link

rationale.decision: User-controllable text sizing for older audiences
44px controls (AAA) on the accessibility widget itself
Announces the new scale via a polite live region

behaviors: data-wire-text-size (init on the widget)
data-wire-text-size-value="sm|md|lg|xl" on each button (A- / A / A+ / A++)
sets --wire-text-scale (0.9/1/1.15/1.3) on <html>, persists to localStorage 'wire-text-scale', aria-pressed on active, polite live-region announcement (initTextSize)
components.notes: Prior manifest's options ['A','A','A+'] were wrong. Behavior exposes four scales sm/md/lg/xl; CSS button modifiers are sm/md/lg.
components.label: Text size

doc_chrome._note: The system's OWN documentation-site furniture (v1.11 M3-IA work). Real and in css/wire.css, but NOT wireframe parts for client composition. Do not use these in generated client pages.

components.notes: Inline component-lifecycle pill. Differentiates by fill density in grayscale.
Two-column sticky-nav + main layout. Superseded as page chrome by the global app-shell (v1.12); retained because it still defines wire-tabs--sticky used by component detail pages.

behaviors: body.wire-shell scopes the chrome so client demo pages (which share wire-topnav) are unaffected

wire-railnav is the persistent left nav at >=64em and the off-canvas slide-over below it — one element, reusing initDrawer via data-wire-drawer-open="site-nav"; modal semantics applied only while open
current page stamped at sync time: aria-current + is-current on the link and open on its section (no JS needed for the highlight)
components.notes: Carbon-style doc-site shell (v1.12): slim fixed header (wire-topnav--shell) + persistent left rail (wire-railnav holding the wire-doc-nav tree) + optional in-page TOC (wire-inpagenav). The horizontal wire-topnav__menu and wire-megamenu are no longer doc-site chrome — megamenu remains a documented nav-family pattern/component. Maintained by scripts/sync-nav.mjs from partials/nav.html.

templates._note: Two kinds. page_shells are abstract reusable recipes (docs/page-shells.html). The rest are concrete pressure-test DEMO pages — verified markup to copy from. The brief's 'four Riverside mocks' is outdated: there are now 18 Riverside (hospital) + 3 Northgate (university) demos. composed_of is derived from each file's real wire-* usage.

shells.intent: Marketing front door — awareness + one clear next step.
Long-form editorial with a table of contents.
spine: inpagenav (sticky) + wire-prose body (quote, callout, media, timeline)

shells.intent: A single nameable entity (provider, condition, lab, program).
spine: inpagenav + tabs / stacked sections (twocol, accordion, deflist)

riverside_mocks._note: Anonymized rehabilitation-hospital demo (Riverside Medical Center). 18 pages. file = the markup to copy.

experiences_status.note: Folded into Templates ▸ Advanced (see nav). The advanced template pages are scaffolded as stubs (status: planned) and composed from existing demo pages until built.

extension_lanes._note: How client divergence is intended to work (PREVIEW-SYSTEM-BRIEF.md §4) vs what exists in source today. Use these lanes; do not invent a parallel mechanism. Both lanes are example/empty today.

styling.intended: css/clients/<client>.css — per-client overlay that re-aliases semantic tokens and adds client-scoped variants; component selectors never change.
styling.today: No css/clients/ directory exists. The pattern ships as a single example template at css/client-overlay.example.css (load AFTER css/wire.css; re-define tokens only). Copy + rename it per engagement.

composition.intended: projects/<client>/ — brief.md + generated <page-id>.html pages. Never edit core to fit one project.
composition.today: No projects/ directory exists yet. No client projects have been generated.

content_model_hint.purpose: Pull realistic placeholder content from the project brief, not lorem ipsum. Generated pages land with clients only when the content is plausible.
